verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens Job, ait :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.king_james: Then Job answered and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Vere scio quod ita sit,  et quod non justificetur homo compositus Deo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אׇ֭מְנָם יָדַ֣עְתִּי כִי־כֵ֑ן וּמַה־יִּצְדַּ֖ק אֱנ֣וֹשׁ עִם־אֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: I know it is so of a truth: but how should man be just with God?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si voluerit contendere cum eo,  non poterit ei respondere unum pro mille.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יַ֭חְפֹּץ לָרִ֣יב עִמּ֑וֹ לֹֽא־יַ֝עֲנֶ֗נּוּ אַחַ֥ת מִנִּי־אָֽלֶף׃
verses.king_james: If he will contend with him, he cannot answer him one of a thousand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sapiens corde est, et fortis robore :  quis restitit ei, et pacem habuit ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֲכַ֣ם לֵ֭בָב וְאַמִּ֣יץ כֹּ֑חַ מִֽי־הִקְשָׁ֥ה אֵ֝לָ֗יו וַיִּשְׁלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: He is wise in heart, and mighty in strength: who hath hardened himself against him, and hath prospered?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui transtulit montes, et nescierunt  hi quos subvertit in furore suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַמַּעְתִּ֣יק הָ֭רִים וְלֹ֣א יָדָ֑עוּ אֲשֶׁ֖ר הֲפָכָ֣ם בְּאַפּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Which removeth the mountains, and they know not: which overturneth them in his anger.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui commovet terram de loco suo,  et columnæ ejus concutiuntur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַמַּרְגִּ֣יז אֶ֭רֶץ מִמְּקוֹמָ֑הּ וְ֝עַמּוּדֶ֗יהָ יִתְפַּלָּצֽוּן׃
verses.king_james: Which shaketh the earth out of her place, and the pillars thereof tremble.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui præcipit soli, et non oritur,  et stellas claudit quasi sub signaculo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָאֹמֵ֣ר לַ֭חֶרֶס וְלֹ֣א יִזְרָ֑ח וּבְעַ֖ד כּוֹכָבִ֣ים יַחְתֹּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Which commandeth the sun, and it riseth not; and sealeth up the stars.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui extendit cælos solus,  et graditur super fluctus maris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֹטֶ֣ה שָׁמַ֣יִם לְבַדּ֑וֹ וְ֝דוֹרֵ֗ךְ עַל־בָּ֥מֳתֵי יָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Which alone spreadeth out the heavens, and treadeth upon the waves of the sea.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui facit Arcturum et Oriona,  et Hyadas et interiora austri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֹֽשֶׂה־עָ֭שׁ כְּסִ֥יל וְכִימָ֗ה וְחַדְרֵ֥י תֵמָֽן׃
verses.king_james: Which maketh Arcturus, Orion, and Pleiades, and the chambers of the south.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui facit magna, et incomprehensibilia,  et mirabilia, quorum non est numerus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֹשֶׂ֣ה גְ֭דֹלוֹת עַד־אֵ֣ין חֵ֑קֶר וְ֝נִפְלָא֗וֹת עַד־אֵ֥ין מִסְפָּֽר׃
verses.king_james: Which doeth great things past finding out; yea, and wonders without number.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si venerit ad me, non videbo eum ;  si abierit, non intelligam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֤ן יַעֲבֹ֣ר עָ֭לַי וְלֹ֣א אֶרְאֶ֑ה וְ֝יַחֲלֹ֗ף וְֽלֹא־אָבִ֥ין לֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Lo, he goeth by me, and I see him not: he passeth on also, but I perceive him not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si repente interroget, quis respondebit ei ?  vel quis dicere potest : Cur ita facis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֣ן יַ֭חְתֹּף מִ֣י יְשִׁיבֶ֑נּוּ מִי־יֹאמַ֥ר אֵ֝לָ֗יו מַֽה־תַּעֲשֶֽׂה׃
verses.king_james: Behold, he taketh away, who can hinder him? who will say unto him, What doest thou?

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus, cujus iræ nemo resistere potest,  et sub quo curvantur qui portant orbem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱ֭לוֹהַּ לֹא־יָשִׁ֣יב אַפּ֑וֹ תַּחְתָּ֥ו שָׁ֝חֲח֗וּ עֹ֣זְרֵי רָֽהַב׃
verses.king_james: If God will not withdraw his anger, the proud helpers do stoop under him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quantus ergo sum ego, ut respondeam ei,  et loquar verbis meis cum eo ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֭ף כִּֽי־אָנֹכִ֣י אֶעֱנֶ֑נּוּ אֶבְחֲרָ֖ה דְבָרַ֣י עִמּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: How much less shall I answer him, and choose out my words to reason with him?

verses.latin_vulgate: qui etiam si habuero quippiam justum, non respondebo :  sed meum judicem deprecabor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֣ר אִם־צָ֭דַקְתִּי לֹ֣א אֶעֱנֶ֑ה לִ֝מְשֹׁפְטִ֗י אֶתְחַנָּֽן׃
verses.king_james: Whom, though I were righteous, yet would I not answer, but I would make supplication to my judge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum invocantem exaudierit me,  non credo quod audierit vocem meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־קָרָ֥אתִי וַֽיַּעֲנֵ֑נִי לֹֽא־אַ֝אֲמִ֗ין כִּֽי־יַאֲזִ֥ין קוֹלִֽי׃
verses.king_james: If I had called, and he had answered me; yet would I not believe that he had hearkened unto my voice.

verses.latin_vulgate: In turbine enim conteret me,  et multiplicabit vulnera mea, etiam sine causa.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁר־בִּשְׂעָרָ֥ה יְשׁוּפֵ֑נִי וְהִרְבָּ֖ה פְצָעַ֣י חִנָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: For he breaketh me with a tempest, and multiplieth my wounds without cause.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non concedit requiescere spiritum meum,  et implet me amaritudinibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹֽא־יִ֭תְּנֵנִי הָשֵׁ֣ב רוּחִ֑י כִּ֥י יַ֝שְׂבִּעַ֗נִי מַמְּרֹרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: He will not suffer me to take my breath, but filleth me with bitterness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si fortitudo quæritur, robustissimus est ;  si æquitas judicii, nemo audet pro me testimonium dicere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־לְכֹ֣חַ אַמִּ֣יץ הִנֵּ֑ה וְאִם־לְ֝מִשְׁפָּ֗ט מִ֣י יוֹעִידֵֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: If I speak of strength, lo, he is strong: and if of judgment, who shall set me a time to plead?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si justificare me voluero, os meum condemnabit me ;  si innocentem ostendero, pravum me comprobabit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אֶ֭צְדָּק פִּ֣י יַרְשִׁיעֵ֑נִי תׇּֽם־אָ֝֗נִי וַֽיַּעְקְשֵֽׁנִי׃
verses.king_james: If I justify myself, mine own mouth shall condemn me: if I say, I am perfect, it shall also prove me perverse.

verses.latin_vulgate: Etiam si simplex fuero, hoc ipsum ignorabit anima mea,  et tædebit me vitæ meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תׇּֽם־אָ֭נִי לֹֽא־אֵדַ֥ע נַפְשִׁ֗י אֶמְאַ֥ס חַיָּֽי׃
verses.king_james: Though I were perfect, yet would I not know my soul: I would despise my life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unum est quod locutus sum :  et innocentem et impium ipse consumit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַחַ֗ת הִ֥֫יא עַל־כֵּ֥ן אָמַ֑רְתִּי תָּ֥ם וְ֝רָשָׁ֗ע ה֣וּא מְכַלֶּֽה׃
verses.king_james: This is one thing, therefore I said it, He destroyeth the perfect and the wicked.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si flagellat, occidat semel,  et non de pœnis innocentum rideat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־שׁ֭וֹט יָמִ֣ית פִּתְאֹ֑ם לְמַסַּ֖ת נְקִיִּ֣ם יִלְעָֽג׃
verses.king_james: If the scourge slay suddenly, he will laugh at the trial of the innocent.

verses.latin_vulgate: Terra data est in manus impii ;  vultum judicum ejus operit.  Quod si non ille est, quis ergo est ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶ֤רֶץ ׀ נִתְּנָ֬ה בְֽיַד־רָשָׁ֗ע פְּנֵֽי־שֹׁפְטֶ֥יהָ יְכַסֶּ֑ה אִם־לֹ֖א אֵפ֣וֹא מִי־הֽוּא׃
verses.king_james: The earth is given into the hand of the wicked: he covereth the faces of the judges thereof; if not, where, and who is he?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dies mei velociores fuerunt cursore ;  fugerunt, et non viderunt bonum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיָמַ֣י קַ֭לּוּ מִנִּי־רָ֑ץ בָּ֝רְח֗וּ לֹא־רָא֥וּ טוֹבָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Now my days are swifter than a post: they flee away, they see no good.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pertransierunt quasi naves poma portantes ;  sicut aquila volans ad escam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָ֭לְפוּ עִם־אֳנִיּ֣וֹת אֵבֶ֑ה כְּ֝נֶ֗שֶׁר יָט֥וּשׂ עֲלֵי־אֹֽכֶל׃
verses.king_james: They are passed away as the swift ships: as the eagle that hasteth to the prey.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum dixero : Nequaquam ita loquar :  commuto faciem meam, et dolore torqueor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אׇ֭מְרִי אֶשְׁכְּחָ֣ה שִׂיחִ֑י אֶעֶזְבָ֖ה פָנַ֣י וְאַבְלִֽיגָה׃
verses.king_james: If I say, I will forget my complaint, I will leave off my heaviness, and comfort myself:

verses.latin_vulgate: Verebar omnia opera mea,  sciens quod non parceres delinquenti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָגֹ֥רְתִּי כׇל־עַצְּבֹתָ֑י יָ֝דַ֗עְתִּי כִּי־לֹ֥א תְנַקֵּֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: I am afraid of all my sorrows, I know that thou wilt not hold me innocent.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem et sic impius sum,  quare frustra laboravi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָנֹכִ֥י אֶרְשָׁ֑ע לָמָּה־זֶּ֝֗ה הֶ֣בֶל אִיגָֽע׃
verses.king_james: If I be wicked, why then labour I in vain?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si lotus fuero quasi aquis nivis,  et fulserint velut mundissimæ manus meæ,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־הִתְרָחַ֥צְתִּי (במו) [בְמֵי־]שָׁ֑לֶג וַ֝הֲזִכּ֗וֹתִי בְּבֹ֣ר כַּפָּֽי׃
verses.king_james: If I wash myself with snow water, and make my hands never so clean;

verses.latin_vulgate: tamen sordibus intinges me,  et abominabuntur me vestimenta mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֭ז בַּשַּׁ֣חַת תִּטְבְּלֵ֑נִי וְ֝תִעֲב֗וּנִי שַׂלְמוֹתָֽי׃
verses.king_james: Yet shalt thou plunge me in the ditch, and mine own clothes shall abhor me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Neque enim viro qui similis mei est, respondebo ;  nec qui mecum in judicio ex æquo possit audiri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־לֹא־אִ֣ישׁ כָּמ֣וֹנִי אֶעֱנֶ֑נּוּ נָב֥וֹא יַ֝חְדָּ֗ו בַּמִּשְׁפָּֽט׃
verses.king_james: For he is not a man, as I am, that I should answer him, and we should come together in judgment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est qui utrumque valeat arguere,  et ponere manum suam in ambobus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹ֣א יֵשׁ־בֵּינֵ֣ינוּ מוֹכִ֑יחַ יָשֵׁ֖ת יָד֣וֹ עַל־שְׁנֵֽינוּ׃
verses.king_james: Neither is there any daysman betwixt us, that might lay his hand upon us both.

verses.latin_vulgate: Auferat a me virgam suam,  et pavor ejus non me terreat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָסֵ֣ר מֵעָלַ֣י שִׁבְט֑וֹ וְ֝אֵמָת֗וֹ אַֽל־תְּבַעֲתַֽנִּי׃
verses.king_james: Let him take his rod away from me, and let not his fear terrify me:

verses.latin_vulgate: Loquar, et non timebo eum ;  neque enim possum metuens respondere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲֽ֭דַבְּרָה וְלֹ֣א אִירָאֶ֑נּוּ כִּ֥י לֹא־כֵ֥ן אָ֝נֹכִ֗י עִמָּדִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Then would I speak, and not fear him; but it is not so with me.
